heading: Restrictive use of information
label: 1736 Restrictive use of information
notes: Effective DateSection effective on first day of sixth month which begins after , see , set out as a note preceding , Postal Service.Aug. 12, 1970section 15(b) of Pub. L. 91–375section 101 of Title 39

children.label: a
children.text: No information or evidence obtained by reason of compliance by a natural person with any provision of , or regulations issued thereunder, shall, except as provided in subsection (c) of this section, be used, directly or indirectly, as evidence against that person in a criminal proceeding.section 3010 of title 39

children.label: b
children.text: The fact of the performance of any act by an individual in compliance with any provision of , or regulations issued thereunder, shall not be deemed the admission of any fact, or otherwise be used, directly or indirectly, as evidence against that person in a criminal proceeding, except as provided in subsection (c) of this section.section 3010 of title 39

children.label: c
children.text: Subsections (a) and (b) of this section shall not preclude the use of any such information or evidence in a prosecution or other action under any applicable provision of law with respect to the furnishing of false information.
